recipe.solutionCode:
-- file: schema.sql
-- Update the warehouses table with a default status
CREATE TABLE warehouses (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active'
);

-- Define the stock_movements table
CREATE TABLE stock_movements (
    id INTEGER,
    warehouse_id INTEGER,
    sku TEXT,
    quantity_change REAL,
    movement_type TEXT,
    note TEXT
);

-- file: query.sql
-- Verify the table creation
SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';

-- file: schema.sql
-- Update the warehouses table with a default status
CREATE TABLE warehouses (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active'
);

-- Update the stock_movements table with constraints
CREATE TABLE stock_movements (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    warehouse_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    sku TEXT NOT NULL,
    quantity_change REAL NOT NULL,
    movement_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    note TEXT
);

recipe.solutionCode:
-- file: schema.sql
-- Update the warehouses table with a default status
CREATE TABLE warehouses (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active'
);

-- Update the stock_movements table with a foreign key
CREATE TABLE stock_movements (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    warehouse_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    sku TEXT NOT NULL,
    quantity_change REAL NOT NULL,
    movement_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'receipt',
    note TEXT,
    FOREIGN KEY (warehouse_id) REFERENCES warehouses(id)
);

-- file: query.sql
-- Verify the foreign key constraint
SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';
recipe.checkSql: SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';
